^Graham: Shouldn't it be on the brink? At the brink, on the brink, is there a difference?
^Graham: Worry about that later, get started.

Title: ^Graham: What'll I call it? Doesn't matter.
^audio = Graham05
end
Quit: ^Graham: I'll implement this later, sorry you're along for the ride.

Brainstorm: ^Graham: Ok come up with some ideas. At the brink, what does that mean? Google.
^Graham: On the verge of doing something or something happening, like I'm at the brink of starting a game.

c-0: ^Graham: A story about someone on life support and their regrets, played out in conversations with visitors.
^Graham: Can people on life support talk? Don't they have that tube thing in their mouth. Maybe a terminal illness then, ugh this is depressing me before I've made it.
c-1: ^Graham: Everyone's going to do that, extra points if it's Lovecraftian. Next.
c-2: ^Graham: Following a comet strike on the Earth, a family of dinosaurs struggle to adapt to the new world, scrap that, it reminds me of that awful Disney film.
c-3: ^Graham: A capable young woman moves to London to enter the world of work. Bright and optimistic, she soon finds frustration as male colleagues undermine, harass, and objectify her.
^Graham: Not sure how this is a game. Maybe some kind of bottling of emotions, let's put it in the maybe pile.
c-4: ^Graham: Like the environment, that's actually quite pertinent, those climate change protests were going on today. I was stuck in traffic, ironically creating more CO2 emissions.
c-5: ^Graham: A shipwrecked man on desert island with no resources must wait for rescue. A game about waiting, that'll be fun.
c-6: ^Graham: There's lots of survival games, so this has promise. No zombies though!

^Graham: A downed helicopter pilot in enemy territory must survive with limited resources while soldiers search for him. Hmm unless I can narrow it down, it's a bit too ambitions for a three day game jam.
c-7: ^Graham: The world's major powers declare nuclear war on each other, I loved that Amiga game, I wonder if I still have it. There was no real narrative though, it's not really suitable for this jam.

Collapse: ^Graham: The environment is on the verge of collapse, scientists have told us so for a long time now.
^Graham: People deny it though, I don't think any scientists do, not reputable one's anyway.

^Graham: Who'd be the protagonist? A protester on their bike? An oil baron? Mother nature? Who needs to change. Mother nature won't change, does that mean she's the antagonist? We need to change to prevent mother nature from destroying us. Destroying us as a reaction to our folly.
^Graham: Why are we as a species moving forward with our own destruction. Greed, denial, stupidity. A combination of those things, then why are we those things?
^Graham: I've got it, I need to start at the beginning, we didn't one day decide to destroy the environment. Bit by bit we bent mother nature to our will, we got here gradually.

^Graham: Now we're carrying on like always because that was what worked in the past, that's what was rewarding, so we're stuck in this pattern of behaviour. How do we collectively realise that and make the change.
^Graham: To make this game I just need to unpack the entire human condition. This game needs to be about everything.

Title2: ^Graham: I'll put a placeholder title for now.

Quit: ^Graham: I'll sort quit out later, I'm on a roll.

CaveManDays: ^Graham: We first begin in cave man days, that's not the proper term, make a note to research the setting later.
^Graham: They are discovering fire for the first time, enabling them to overcome the cold and keep predators at bay.
^Graham: Wait, what the hell am I doing, I've only got two and a half days left and I'm trying to get the whole of human history into a game.
^Graham: Think. Narrow it down. One person, and what they can do on the individual level.
^Graham: Like those protesters, they were doing their bit. You even admired them as you sat in your car creating more emissions. You weren't going anywhere you could've turned your engine off.
Title3: ^Graham: Okay, title screen. Car game placeholder.

Quit: ^Graham: Quit doesn't work, get over it.

CarGameIntro: ^Graham: You're trying to get to the Bristol Hippodrome to watch the Lion King, an accident on the M4 meant you were already running behind, but now you're in heavy traffic.
^Graham: Climate change protesters are bringing the city centre to a standstill, you need to control your emissions as you slowly move forward.
^Graham: Everytime you stop, switch off the engine.
AfterLionKing: ^Graham: You watched Lion King, it was great but you're aware that inkJam 2019 has already started. You wonder what the theme is.

c-0: ^Graham: At the brink, how is that a theme? It's more like a writing prompt.
^Graham: I have to drive back to Exeter now anyway, I'll use that time to come up with some ideas. Then I'll get some sleep and start in the morning.

Drive: ^Graham: I'll drive into the cups to collect the precious elixir of alertness.

FirstCup: ^Graham: These are paper cups, I don't know why they can't be recycled, but this game is about the environment!
Mugs: ^Graham: Okay whatever, actual mugs of coffee will do.

Home: ^Graham: Sleep, start the game in the morning.

g-0: ^Graham: I guess we're just gonna think about game ideas and this theme. Who starts a game jam at night, that's so cruel. They know we won't be able to sleep, all that creative energy is at a maximum now.
^Graham: Paul even put in the email don't forget to get some sleep, that arsehole knew exactly what he was doing!

Start: ^Graham: At the brink of environmental collapse, that's my game, that's the theme, but what's the story? This is a narrative game jam after all.
^Graham: I'll look at other environmental stories and see if they can give me some ideas.

c-0: ^Graham: The clash of civilisation and nature, the humans exploit the resources of mother nature. Mother nature fights back. Eboshi desires to destroy the forest, she goes on a hunting expedition to kill the Forest Spirit.
^Graham: When it's decapitated, ooze comes out destroying everything it touches. Mother nature's revenge for humankind's greed.
c-1: ^Graham: We come to another planet to mine for minerals, but there's a civilisation that lives in harmony with nature opposing us. Jake becomes an avatar living among them, like some kind of hearts and minds mission.
^Graham: Jake learns that there is another way of living that doesn't have to be destructive and unsustainable, and that's worth protecting.
c-2: ^Graham: A penguin named Mumble is born, due to an accident he can't do the mating call of penguins, but he can tap dance. Ridiculed by his elders, the conventional wisdom, he feels isolated and eventually ends up in captivity.
^Graham: Mumble becomes popular with his dancing, and is set free with a tracking device. Humans come to his home and realise their actions have caused harm to the penguins and change their ways.
^Graham: We learn that new and different voices need to be listened to if we are going to save the future.
c-3: ^Graham: What am I doing? It's night time already and you've just been going down all these rabbit holes. You even watched Happy Feet for the first time so you'd have something to write, all because you didn't want two Ghibli films in the options, but that would've been fine.

^Graham: I don't know if I can finish this, it's okay no one will notice if you don't submit anything. Just put a heroic message on Discord about how the scope got out of control, you learned so much and are looking forward to next year.
^Graham: Go to sleep, we'll have ice cream tomorrow.

^Jon: You ask him to do that later!

^Graham: My game, inkJam. I don't know what to do you've got to save me.

s: ^Graham: It doesn't matter, just give me some good advice.

c-0: ^ and how it's on the brink of collapse, and well, I don't really know how to make that into a game.

s: ^Graham: Because they feel there's a right or wrong

g-1: ^Jon: Nice try but that was just yes, no, and no but yes.

Continue: ^Graham: Cut to brainstorming session, imagined prototypes, our game developer is anxious to get started. But, we've got the long midnight drive back to Devon.
Radio: ^Newsreader: Reports of a giant tentacled monster are coming out Exeter this evening. The beast is beached over the whole of Exmouth and its tentacles are striking out at Exeter and nearby villages. Mass casualties are expected.
^Newsreader: Experts are calling this the coming of Cthulhu, some speculating that pollution of the seas has caused him to act and seek revenge on us all.
^Newsreader: The government warns against all unnecessary travel and to remain in your homes, but in this newsreader's humble opinion, run! Get away from Cthulhu as fast as you can!
